articles.title: ExpressVPN drops to $2.79/mo: A great deal on our favorite VPN
articles.description: NOW 78% OFF ExpressVPN 2-year plan + 4 months View Deal Online privacy has never been more important, and if you ask me, everyone should have a VPN installed on their devices. ExpressVPN has long been one of the best options, but admittedly hard to swallow for its higher-than-average prices. But right now, you can snag ExpressVPN on sale for as little as $78 for a 2-year plan with 4 extra months . That’s a 78% discount that comes out to just $2.79 per month. Check out our ExpressVPN review to see why we gave it 4.5 stars and our Editors’ Choice award. Even the Basic plan offers a lot, including coverage for up to 10 devices, ad blocking, protection against malicious sites, and a private email relay for up to 10 email addresses. ExpressVPN works on Windows, macOS, Linux, Chrome OS, Android, iOS, and even some routers, streaming sticks, and gaming consoles. In addition to guarding your online privacy, ExpressVPN is one of the best for bypassing regional blocks on streaming sites like Netflix, and you can route your connection through hundreds of servers across 105 countries. ExpressVPN offers a 30-day money-back guarantee for new users, so rest assured that you can give it a go for nearly a month and still get a full refund if you decide you don’t want it after all. Some key features missing in the Basic plan include blocking of trackers and adult sites, password management, 2FA authentication, and monitoring of various personal data leaks like social security numbers, credit reports, home and auto titles, and more. For those, you’ll need to bump up to the Advanced ($3.59/mo) or Pro ($5.99/mo) plans. There’s a good reason it tops PCWorld’s picks for the best VPNs . It’ll be hard to find an equivalent VPN for less, but you can check out our roundup of the best VPN deals if you want to shop around.

articles.title: Lebanon: Middle East Escalation, LTC Situation Report (Telecoms) #05 - Reporting period: 15 – 30 April 2026
articles.description: Country: Lebanon Sources: Emergency Telecommunications Cluster, Logistics Cluster, World Food Programme Please refer to the attached file. This situation report provides updates on the Middle East escalation, with focus on Lebanon’s security situation. It outlines the telecoms area activities of the Logistics & Telecommunications Cluster (LTC) in support of the humanitarian response. The Cluster was formally activated on 16 March 2026 and is led by the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP). Summary Points • Network congestion in the north has sharply increased as the population has doubled due to displacement. Many people sheltering in accommodation centres have no reliable internet access to communicate with their families, and mobile networks in these areas are heavily overloaded. • The Government has announced that Starlink service will soon be activated, with priority access in the initial phase for government institutions, hospitals, and other critical services. • LTC continues to lead coordination of telecom needs, including working with mobile network operators on site restoration, expanding internet speed-testing through humanitarian convoys, and supporting convoy-tracking systems to strengthen staff safety and security.
